description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed graphic with a near-black background. The top half is a photograph of President Donald Trump standing at an outdoor lectern (with a green colonnade and an American flag visible behind him), holding up an open leather-bound presidential document/executive order so the camera can see his signature on the right-hand page. Overlaid across the middle in white serif type (italic + bold) is the slogan 'Prioritizing AMERICANS!'. Below sits a white rounded rectangle card styled like a news excerpt: it carries 'The Washington Post' masthead in its blackletter logo font, then two paragraphs of black body text reading: 'The Trump administration said it will launch investigations into employers who take advantage of the H-1B visa program for highly skilled workers by hiring low-skilled foreign labor to cut costs.' and 'The U.S. Labor Department said it would begin opening investigations of employers that "abuse" the H-1B visa program, as part of a broader effort to prioritize Americans over foreign-born workers in the labor market.' The technique combines an authoritative presidential image with a mainstream-press quotation to legitimize the H-1B crackdown messaging. Target audience: American workers and the MAGA base, framing the administration as protecting U.S. jobs from foreign-labor abuse.
notable_text: Prioritizing AMERICANS! | The Washington Post: The Trump administration said it will launch investigations into employers who take advantage of the H-1B visa program... to prioritize Americans over foreign-born workers in the labor market.
